    96→    @Get
    97→    @Transactional(readOnly = true)
    98→    open fun listDemands(@QueryValue @Nullable status: DemandStatus?,
    99→                        @QueryValue @Nullable demandType: DemandType?,
   100→                        @QueryValue @Nullable priority: Priority?,
   101→                        @QueryValue @Nullable requestorId: Long?): HttpResponse<List<Demand>> {
   102→        return try {
   103→            log.debug("Fetching demands with filters - status: {}, type: {}, priority: {}, requestor: {}", 
   104→                     status, demandType, priority, requestorId)
   105→            
   106→            val demands = if (status != null || demandType != null || priority != null || requestorId != null) {
   107→                demandRepository.findWithFilters(status, demandType, priority, requestorId)
   108→            } else {
   109→                entityManager.createQuery(
   110→                    """
   111→                    SELECT DISTINCT d FROM Demand d 
   112→                    LEFT JOIN FETCH d.requestor 
   113→                    LEFT JOIN FETCH d.approver 
   114→                    LEFT JOIN FETCH d.existingAsset 
   115→                    ORDER BY d.requestedDate DESC
   116→                    """,
   117→                    Demand::class.java
   118→                ).resultList
   119→            }
   120→            
   121→            log.debug("Found {} demands", demands.size)
   122→            HttpResponse.ok(demands)
   123→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   124→            log.error("Error fetching demands", e)
   125→            HttpResponse.serverError<List<Demand>>()
   126→        }
   127→    }
   128→
   129→    @Get("/{id}")
   130→    @Transactional(readOnly = true)
   131→    open fun getDemand(id: Long): HttpResponse<*> {
   132→        return try {
   133→            log.debug("Fetching demand with id: {}", id)
   134→            
   135→            val demand = entityManager.createQuery(
   136→                """
   137→                SELECT d FROM Demand d 
   138→                LEFT JOIN FETCH d.requestor 
   139→                LEFT JOIN FETCH d.approver 
   140→                LEFT JOIN FETCH d.existingAsset 
   141→                WHERE d.id = :id
   142→                """,
   143→                Demand::class.java
   144→            ).setParameter("id", id).resultList.firstOrNull()
   145→            
   146→            if (demand != null) {
   147→                log.debug("Found demand: {}", demand.id)
   148→                HttpResponse.ok(demand)
   149→            } else {
   150→                log.debug("Demand not found with id: {}", id)
   151→                HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse("NOT_FOUND", "Demand not found"))
   152→            }
   153→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   154→            log.error("Error fetching demand with id: {}", id, e)
   155→            HttpResponse.serverError<Any>()
   156→        }
   157→    }
   158→
   159→    @Get("/summary")
   160→    @Transactional(readOnly = true)
   161→    open fun getDemandSummary(): HttpResponse<DemandSummary> {
   162→        return try {
   163→            log.debug("Generating demand summary")
   164→            
   165→            val summary = DemandSummary(
   166→                totalDemands = demandRepository.count(),
   167→                pendingDemands = demandRepository.countByStatus(DemandStatus.PENDING),
   168→                approvedDemands = demandRepository.countByStatus(DemandStatus.APPROVED),
   169→                rejectedDemands = demandRepository.countByStatus(DemandStatus.REJECTED),
   170→                changeDemands = demandRepository.countByDemandType(DemandType.CHANGE),
   171→                createNewDemands = demandRepository.countByDemandType(DemandType.CREATE_NEW)
   172→            )
   173→            
   174→            log.debug("Generated demand summary: {}", summary)
   175→            HttpResponse.ok(summary)

   182→    @Get("/approved/available")
   183→    @Transactional(readOnly = true)
   184→    open fun getApprovedDemandsForRiskAssessment(): HttpResponse<List<Demand>> {
   185→        return try {
   186→            log.debug("Fetching approved demands available for risk assessment")
   187→            
   188→            val demands = demandRepository.findApprovedDemandsWithoutRiskAssessment()
   189→            
   190→            // Force loading of related entities
   191→            demands.forEach { demand ->
   192→                demand.requestor.username
   193→                demand.approver?.username
   194→                demand.existingAsset?.name
   195→            }
   196→            
   197→            log.debug("Found {} approved demands available for risk assessment", demands.size)
   198→            HttpResponse.ok(demands)
   199→        } catch (e: Exception) {

   201→            HttpResponse.serverError<List<Demand>>()
   202→        }

   205→    @Post
   206→    @Transactional
   207→    open fun createDemand(@Valid @Body request: CreateDemandRequest): HttpResponse<*> {
   208→        return try {
   209→            log.debug("Creating demand: {}", request.title)
   210→            
   211→            // Validate requestor exists
   212→            val requestor = userRepository.findById(request.requestorId).orElse(null)
   213→                ?: return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("VALIDATION_ERROR", "Requestor not found"))
   214→            
   215→            // Validate asset information based on demand type
   216→            val existingAsset = if (request.demandType == DemandType.CHANGE) {
   217→                request.existingAssetId?.let { assetId ->
   218→                    assetRepository.findById(assetId).orElse(null)
   219→                        ?: return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("VALIDATION_ERROR", "Existing asset not found"))
   220→                } ?: return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("VALIDATION_ERROR", "Existing asset ID required for CHANGE demand"))
   221→            } else null
   222→            
   223→            // Validate new asset information for CREATE_NEW demands
   224→            if (request.demandType == DemandType.CREATE_NEW) {
   225→                if (request.newAssetName.isNullOrBlank() || 
   226→                    request.newAssetType.isNullOrBlank() || 
   227→                    request.newAssetOwner.isNullOrBlank()) {
   228→                    return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("VALIDATION_ERROR", 
   229→                        "New asset name, type, and owner are required for CREATE_NEW demand"))
   230→                }
   231→            }
   232→            
   233→            // Create demand
   234→            val demand = Demand(
   235→                title = request.title.trim(),
   236→                description = request.description?.trim()?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   237→                demandType = request.demandType,
   238→                existingAsset = existingAsset,
   239→                newAssetName = request.newAssetName?.trim()?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   240→                newAssetType = request.newAssetType?.trim()?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   241→                newAssetIp = request.newAssetIp?.trim()?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   242→                newAssetOwner = request.newAssetOwner?.trim()?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   243→                newAssetDescription = request.newAssetDescription?.trim()?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   244→                businessJustification = request.businessJustification?.trim()?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   245→                priority = request.priority ?: Priority.MEDIUM,
   246→                requestor = requestor
   247→            )
   248→            
   249→            val savedDemand = demandRepository.save(demand)
   250→            
   251→            // Fetch the complete demand with all relationships
   252→            val completeDemand = entityManager.createQuery(
   253→                """
   254→                SELECT d FROM Demand d 
   255→                LEFT JOIN FETCH d.requestor 
   256→                LEFT JOIN FETCH d.existingAsset 
   257→                WHERE d.id = :id
   258→                """,
   259→                Demand::class.java
   260→            ).setParameter("id", savedDemand.id)
   261→             .singleResult
   262→            
   263→            log.info("Created demand with id: {}", completeDemand.id)
   264→            HttpResponse.status<Demand>(HttpStatus.CREATED).body(completeDemand)
   265→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   266→            log.error("Error creating demand", e)
   267→            HttpResponse.serverError<Any>()
   268→        }
   269→    }
   270→
   271→    @Put("/{id}")
   272→    @Transactional
   273→    open fun updateDemand(id: Long, @Valid @Body request: UpdateDemandRequest): HttpResponse<*> {
   274→        return try {
   275→            log.debug("Updating demand with id: {}", id)
   276→            
   277→            val demand = demandRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   278→                ?: return HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse("NOT_FOUND", "Demand not found"))
   279→            
   280→            // Update fields if provided
   281→            request.title?.let { demand.title = it.trim() }
   282→            request.description?.let { demand.description = it.trim().takeIf { it.isNotBlank() } }
   283→            request.businessJustification?.let { demand.businessJustification = it.trim().takeIf { it.isNotBlank() } }
   284→            request.priority?.let { demand.priority = it }
   285→            request.status?.let { demand.status = it }
   286→            
   287→            // Update asset information based on demand type
   288→            if (demand.demandType == DemandType.CHANGE) {
   289→                request.existingAssetId?.let { assetId ->
   290→                    val asset = assetRepository.findById(assetId).orElse(null)
   291→                        ?: return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("VALIDATION_ERROR", "Asset not found"))
   292→                    demand.existingAsset = asset
   293→                }
   294→            } else if (demand.demandType == DemandType.CREATE_NEW) {
   295→                request.newAssetName?.let { demand.newAssetName = it.trim().takeIf { it.isNotBlank() } }
   296→                request.newAssetType?.let { demand.newAssetType = it.trim().takeIf { it.isNotBlank() } }
   297→                request.newAssetIp?.let { demand.newAssetIp = it.trim().takeIf { it.isNotBlank() } }
   298→                request.newAssetOwner?.let { demand.newAssetOwner = it.trim().takeIf { it.isNotBlank() } }
   299→                request.newAssetDescription?.let { demand.newAssetDescription = it.trim().takeIf { it.isNotBlank() } }
   300→            }
   301→            
   302→            val updatedDemand = demandRepository.update(demand)
   303→            
   304→            // Fetch the complete demand with all relationships
   305→            val completeDemand = entityManager.createQuery(
   306→                """
   307→                SELECT d FROM Demand d 
   308→                LEFT JOIN FETCH d.requestor 
   309→                LEFT JOIN FETCH d.approver 
   310→                LEFT JOIN FETCH d.existingAsset 
   311→                WHERE d.id = :id
   312→                """,
   313→                Demand::class.java
   314→            ).setParameter("id", updatedDemand.id)
   315→             .singleResult
   316→            
   317→            log.info("Updated demand with id: {}", id)
   318→            HttpResponse.ok(completeDemand)
   319→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   320→            log.error("Error updating demand with id: {}", id, e)
   321→            HttpResponse.serverError<Any>()
   322→        }
   323→    }
   324→
   325→    @Post("/{id}/approve")
   326→    @Transactional
   327→    open fun approveDemand(id: Long, @Valid @Body request: ApproveDemandRequest): HttpResponse<*> {
   328→        return try {
   329→            log.debug("Processing approval for demand: {}", id)
   330→            
   331→            val demand = demandRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   332→                ?: return HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse("NOT_FOUND", "Demand not found"))
   333→            
   334→            if (demand.status != DemandStatus.PENDING) {
   335→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("INVALID_STATE", 
   336→                    "Only pending demands can be approved or rejected"))
   337→            }
   338→            
   339→            // TODO: Get current user from security context
   340→            // For now, we'll need to pass approver ID or get from context
   341→            // This is a simplification - in production, you'd get the approver from the security context
   342→            
   343→            if (request.approved) {
   344→                demand.status = DemandStatus.APPROVED
   345→                demand.approvedDate = LocalDateTime.now()
   346→                demand.rejectionReason = null
   347→                log.info("Approved demand: {}", id)
   348→            } else {
   349→                demand.status = DemandStatus.REJECTED
   350→                demand.rejectionReason = request.rejectionReason?.trim()?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   351→                    ?: "No reason provided"
   352→                log.info("Rejected demand: {} with reason: {}", id, demand.rejectionReason)
   353→            }
   354→            
   355→            val updatedDemand = demandRepository.update(demand)
   356→            
   357→            // Fetch the complete demand with all relationships
   358→            val completeDemand = entityManager.createQuery(
   359→                """
   360→                SELECT d FROM Demand d 
   361→                LEFT JOIN FETCH d.requestor 
   362→                LEFT JOIN FETCH d.approver 
   363→                LEFT JOIN FETCH d.existingAsset 
   364→                WHERE d.id = :id
   365→                """,
   366→                Demand::class.java
   367→            ).setParameter("id", updatedDemand.id)
   368→             .singleResult
   369→            
   370→            HttpResponse.ok(completeDemand)
   371→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   372→            log.error("Error processing approval for demand: {}", id, e)
   373→            HttpResponse.serverError<Any>()
   374→        }
   375→    }
   376→
   377→    @Delete("/{id}")
   378→    @Transactional
   379→    open fun deleteDemand(id: Long): HttpResponse<*> {
   380→        return try {
   381→            log.debug("Deleting demand with id: {}", id)
   382→            
   383→            val demand = demandRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   384→                ?: return HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse("NOT_FOUND", "Demand not found"))
   385→            
   386→            // Check if demand has associated risk assessments
   387→            val hasRiskAssessments = entityManager.createQuery(
   388→                "SELECT COUNT(ra) FROM RiskAssessment ra WHERE ra.demand.id = :demandId",
   389→                Long::class.java
   390→            ).setParameter("demandId", id).singleResult > 0
   391→            
   392→            if (hasRiskAssessments) {
   393→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("VALIDATION_ERROR", 
   394→                    "Cannot delete demand with associated risk assessments"))
   395→            }
   396→            
   397→            demandRepository.delete(demand)
   398→            
   399→            log.info("Deleted demand with id: {}", id)
   400→            HttpResponse.ok(mapOf("message" to "Demand deleted successfully"))
   401→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   402→            log.error("Error deleting demand with id: {}", id, e)
   403→            HttpResponse.serverError<Any>()
   404→        }
   405→    }
   406→}

    10→@Entity
    11→@Table(
    12→    name = "risk_assessment",
    13→    indexes = [
    14→        Index(name = "idx_risk_assessment_basis", columnList = "assessment_basis_type, assessment_basis_id"),
    15→        Index(name = "idx_risk_assessment_assessor", columnList = "assessor_id"),
    16→        Index(name = "idx_risk_assessment_requestor", columnList = "requestor_id"),
    17→        Index(name = "idx_risk_assessment_status", columnList = "status"),
    18→        Index(name = "idx_risk_assessment_dates", columnList = "start_date, end_date")
    19→    ]
    20→)
    21→@Serdeable
    22→data class RiskAssessment(
    23→    @Id
    24→    @GeneratedValue
    25→    var id: Long? = null,
    26→
    27→    @Column(name = "start_date", nullable = false)
    28→    @NotNull
    29→    var startDate: LocalDate,
    30→
    31→    @Column(name = "end_date", nullable = false)
    32→    @NotNull
    33→    var endDate: LocalDate,
    34→
    35→    @Column(name = "status", length = 50)
    36→    var status: String = "STARTED",
    37→
    38→    @Column(name = "notes", length = 1024)
    39→    var notes: String? = null,
    40→
    41→    @Column(name = "is_release_locked")
    42→    var isReleaseLocked: Boolean = false,
    43→
    44→    @Column(name = "content_snapshot_taken")
    45→    var contentSnapshotTaken: Boolean = false,
    46→
    47→    @Column(name = "release_locked_at")
    48→    var releaseLockedAt: LocalDateTime? = null,
    49→
    50→    // New unified approach: Assessment basis can be either DEMAND or ASSET
    51→    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)
    52→    @Column(name = "assessment_basis_type", nullable = false)
    53→    @NotNull
    54→    var assessmentBasisType: AssessmentBasisType,
    55→
    56→    @Column(name = "assessment_basis_id", nullable = false)
    57→    @NotNull
    58→    var assessmentBasisId: Long,
    59→
    60→    // Legacy fields for backward compatibility during migration
    61→    @ManyToOne
    62→    @JoinColumn(name = "demand_id")
    63→    @Deprecated("Use assessmentBasisType and assessmentBasisId instead. This field is kept for migration compatibility.")
    64→    var demand: Demand? = null,
    65→
    66→    @ManyToOne
    67→    @JoinColumn(name = "asset_id")
    68→    @Deprecated("Use assessmentBasisType and assessmentBasisId instead. This field is kept for migration compatibility.")
    69→    var asset: Asset? = null,
    70→
    71→    @ManyToOne
    72→    @JoinColumn(name = "assessor_id", nullable = false)
    73→    @NotNull
    74→    var assessor: User,
    75→
    76→    @ManyToOne
    77→    @JoinColumn(name = "requestor_id", nullable = false)
    78→    @NotNull
    79→    var requestor: User,
    80→
    81→    @ManyToOne
    82→    @JoinColumn(name = "respondent_id")
    83→    var respondent: User? = null,
    84→
    85→    @ManyToOne
    86→    @JoinColumn(name = "release_id")
    87→    @JsonIgnore
    88→    var lockedRelease: Release? = null,
    89→
    90→    @ManyToMany
    91→    @JoinTable(
    92→        name = "risk_assessment_usecase",
    93→        joinColumns = [JoinColumn(name = "risk_assessment_id")],
    94→        inverseJoinColumns = [JoinColumn(name = "usecase_id")]
    95→    )
    96→    var useCases: MutableSet<UseCase> = mutableSetOf(),
    97→
    98→    @Column(name = "created_at", nullable = false, updatable = false)
    99→    var createdAt: LocalDateTime? = null,
   100→
   101→    @Column(name = "updated_at", nullable = false)
   102→    var updatedAt: LocalDateTime? = null
   103→) {
   104→    // Primary constructor for demand-based risk assessments
   105→    constructor(
   106→        startDate: LocalDate,
   107→        endDate: LocalDate,
   108→        demand: Demand,
   109→        assessor: User,
   110→        requestor: User
   111→    ) : this(
   112→        startDate = startDate,
   113→        endDate = endDate,
   114→        assessmentBasisType = AssessmentBasisType.DEMAND,
   115→        assessmentBasisId = demand.id ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Demand must have an ID"),
   116→        assessor = assessor,
   117→        requestor = requestor,
   118→        status = "STARTED",
   119→        demand = demand,
   120→        asset = null
   121→    )
   122→
   123→    // Constructor for asset-based risk assessments
   124→    constructor(
   125→        startDate: LocalDate,
   126→        endDate: LocalDate,
   127→        asset: Asset,
   128→        assessor: User,
   129→        requestor: User
   130→    ) : this(
   131→        startDate = startDate,
   132→        endDate = endDate,
   133→        assessmentBasisType = AssessmentBasisType.ASSET,
   134→        assessmentBasisId = asset.id ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset must have an ID"),
   135→        assessor = assessor,
   136→        requestor = requestor,
   137→        status = "STARTED",
   138→        demand = null,
   139→        asset = asset
   140→    )
   141→
   142→    @PrePersist
   143→    fun onCreate() {
   144→        val now = LocalDateTime.now()
   145→        createdAt = now
   146→        updatedAt = now
   147→    }
   148→
   149→    @PreUpdate
   150→    fun onUpdate() {
   151→        updatedAt = LocalDateTime.now()
   152→    }
   153→
   154→    /**
   155→     * Gets the demand associated with this risk assessment if basis type is DEMAND
   156→     */
   157→    @Suppress("DEPRECATION")
   158→    fun getDemandBasis(): Demand? {
   159→        return when (assessmentBasisType) {
   160→            AssessmentBasisType.DEMAND -> demand ?: throw IllegalStateException("Demand not loaded for DEMAND basis type")
   161→            AssessmentBasisType.ASSET -> null
   162→        }
   163→    }
   164→
   165→    /**
   166→     * Gets the asset associated with this risk assessment if basis type is ASSET
   167→     */
   168→    @Suppress("DEPRECATION")
   169→    fun getAssetBasis(): Asset? {
   170→        return when (assessmentBasisType) {
   171→            AssessmentBasisType.ASSET -> asset ?: throw IllegalStateException("Asset not loaded for ASSET basis type")
   172→            AssessmentBasisType.DEMAND -> null
   173→        }
   174→    }
   175→
   176→    /**
   177→     * Gets the actual asset for this risk assessment, considering both demand and direct asset basis
   178→     */
   179→    fun getAssociatedAsset(): Asset? {
   180→        return when (assessmentBasisType) {
   181→            AssessmentBasisType.DEMAND -> {
   182→                val demandBasis = getDemandBasis()
   183→                when (demandBasis?.demandType) {
   184→                    DemandType.CHANGE -> demandBasis.existingAsset
   185→                    DemandType.CREATE_NEW -> null // New assets don't exist yet
   186→                    null -> null
   187→                }
   188→            }
   189→            AssessmentBasisType.ASSET -> getAssetBasis()
   190→        }
   191→    }
   192→
   193→    /**
   194→     * Gets the asset name for display purposes
   195→     */
   196→    fun getAssetName(): String {
   197→        return when (assessmentBasisType) {
   198→            AssessmentBasisType.DEMAND -> getDemandBasis()?.getAssetName() ?: "Unknown Asset"
   199→            AssessmentBasisType.ASSET -> getAssetBasis()?.name ?: "Unknown Asset"
   200→        }
   201→    }
   202→
   203→    /**
   204→     * Gets the asset type for display purposes
   205→     */
   206→    fun getAssetType(): String {
   207→        return when (assessmentBasisType) {
   208→            AssessmentBasisType.DEMAND -> getDemandBasis()?.getAssetType() ?: "Unknown"
   209→            AssessmentBasisType.ASSET -> getAssetBasis()?.type ?: "Unknown"
   210→        }
   211→    }
   212→
   213→    /**
   214→     * Gets the description of what is being assessed
   215→     */
   216→    fun getBasisDescription(): String {
   217→        return when (assessmentBasisType) {
   218→            AssessmentBasisType.DEMAND -> getDemandBasis()?.title ?: "Unknown Demand"
   219→            AssessmentBasisType.ASSET -> getAssetBasis()?.name ?: "Unknown Asset"
   220→        }
   221→    }
   222→
   223→    /**
   224→     * Gets the owner for display purposes
   225→     */
   226→    fun getAssetOwner(): String {
   227→        return when (assessmentBasisType) {
   228→            AssessmentBasisType.DEMAND -> getDemandBasis()?.getAssetOwner() ?: "Unknown"
   229→            AssessmentBasisType.ASSET -> getAssetBasis()?.owner ?: "Unknown"
   230→        }
   231→    }
   232→
   233→    /**
   234→     * Validates that the assessment basis configuration is consistent
   235→     */
   236→    @Suppress("DEPRECATION")
   237→    fun validateBasisConsistency(): Boolean {
   238→        return when (assessmentBasisType) {
   239→            AssessmentBasisType.DEMAND -> {
   240→                demand != null &&
   241→                demand?.id == assessmentBasisId &&
   242→                asset == null
   243→            }
   244→            AssessmentBasisType.ASSET -> {
   245→                asset != null &&
   246→                asset?.id == assessmentBasisId &&
   247→                demand == null
   248→            }
   249→        }
   250→    }
   251→
   252→    /**
   253→     * Gets validation errors for the assessment basis configuration
   254→     */
   255→    @Suppress("DEPRECATION")
   256→    fun getBasisValidationErrors(): List<String> {
   257→        val errors = mutableListOf<String>()
   258→
   259→        when (assessmentBasisType) {
   260→            AssessmentBasisType.DEMAND -> {
   261→                if (demand == null) {
   262→                    errors.add("Demand entity must be loaded for DEMAND basis type")
   263→                }
   264→                if (demand?.id != assessmentBasisId) {
   265→                    errors.add("Demand ID (${demand?.id}) must match assessmentBasisId ($assessmentBasisId)")
   266→                }
   267→                if (asset != null) {
   268→                    errors.add("Asset must be null for DEMAND basis type")
   269→                }
   270→            }
   271→            AssessmentBasisType.ASSET -> {
   272→                if (asset == null) {
   273→                    errors.add("Asset entity must be loaded for ASSET basis type")
   274→                }
   275→                if (asset?.id != assessmentBasisId) {
   276→                    errors.add("Asset ID (${asset?.id}) must match assessmentBasisId ($assessmentBasisId)")
   277→                }
   278→                if (demand != null) {
   279→                    errors.add("Demand must be null for ASSET basis type")
   280→                }
   281→            }
   282→        }
   283→
   284→        return errors
   285→    }
   286→
   287→    override fun toString(): String {
   288→        return "RiskAssessment(id=$id, startDate=$startDate, endDate=$endDate, status='$status', basisType=$assessmentBasisType, basis=${getBasisDescription()})"
   289→    }
   290→
   291→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
   292→        if (this === other) return true
   293→        if (other !is RiskAssessment) return false
   294→        return id != null && id == other.id
   295→    }
   296→
   297→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
   298→        return id?.hashCode() ?: 0
   299→    }
   300→}

Good. I have all the context I need. The `RiskAssessment` has `assessor`, `requestor`, and `respondent` user fields — these are the ownership fields for access control. Now let me apply all 5 fixes.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
**IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference)** is one of the most common web vulnerabilities. It occurs when an application exposes internal object IDs (like database primary keys) in URLs without verifying the requesting user has access. The pattern to fix it is consistent: inject `Authentication`, resolve the current user, check access before operating on the resource. The existing `assetFilterService.canAccessAsset()` already implements this correctly for GET — we just need to use it on PUT and ports endpoints too.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

content.text:
**BUILD SUCCESSFUL** — all 54 tasks passed. All 5 P0 vulnerability fixes compile and tests pass.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
**Fix patterns applied across the 5 vulnerabilities:**

1. **Vulns 1 & 2 (Asset IDOR)**: Added `authentication: Authentication` parameter and called the existing `assetFilterService.canAccessAsset()` check. This reuses the same access control logic already correctly implemented for `GET /api/assets/{id}`. For the update endpoint, workgroup reassignment is now restricted to ADMIN-only — preventing a user from reassigning assets to their own workgroups to gain visibility.

2. **Vuln 3 (Risk Assessment IDOR)**: Replaced the `return true` stub with actual ownership checks against `assessor`, `requestor`, and `respondent` fields on the `RiskAssessment` entity. Admins retain full access.

3. **Vuln 4 (MCP Domain Bypass)**: The username path now looks up the user's email and validates it against `isDelegationAllowedForEmail()` — the same check the email path uses. Users without an email are rejected entirely since their domain cannot be verified.

4. **Vuln 5 (Demand Approval)**: Added `@Secured("ADMIN")`, injected `Authentication`, set `demand.approver` from the security context, and added self-approval prevention.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

### Summary of P0 Fixes

| Vuln | File | Fix |
|------|------|-----|
| 1 | `AssetController.kt:202` | Added `authentication` param + `canAccessAsset()` check to PUT; restricted workgroup reassignment to ADMIN |
| 2 | `AssetController.kt:444` | Added `authentication` param + `canAccessAsset()` check to GET ports |
| 3 | `SecurityService.kt:81` | Replaced `return true` with assessor/requestor/respondent ownership checks |
| 4 | `McpDelegationService.kt:170` | Username path now resolves user email and validates against allowed domains |
| 5 | `DemandController.kt:325` | Added `@Secured("ADMIN")`, set approver from auth context, blocked self-approval |

Now let me address P2 (Vulns 11, 12, 13) as you requested.

---

### Fix P2: Vulns 11, 12, 13 — Missing role restrictions

Let me read the files.

**BUILD SUCCESSFUL** — all P2 fixes compile and tests pass.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
**Vuln 11 (Demand requestor spoofing)**: The `createDemand` endpoint previously accepted `requestorId` from the request body, allowing any user to create demands that appear to come from someone else. The fix ignores the body field and derives the requestor from `authentication.attributes["userId"]` — the server-side JWT claim. For update, only the requestor or ADMIN can modify. Delete requires ADMIN.

**Vuln 12 (Risk CRUD)**: The `RiskAssessmentController` already had `@Secured("ADMIN", "RISK", "SECCHAMPION")`, but `RiskController` (managing individual risks) used only `IS_AUTHENTICATED`. This was a gap — the assessment is protected but the risks within it weren't. Now consistent.

**Vuln 13 (Import endpoints)**: The pattern was already established — user-mapping imports had `@Secured("ADMIN")` but other imports didn't. Now: requirement imports require ADMIN/REQADMIN, vulnerability/scan/asset imports require ADMIN/VULN. This matches the role semantics — VULN role users handle vulnerability data, REQADMIN handles requirements.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

### Summary of all fixes applied

| Priority | Vuln | File | Fix |
|----------|------|------|-----|
| **P0** | 1 | `AssetController.kt` | Access control on PUT + ADMIN-only workgroup reassignment |
| **P0** | 2 | `AssetController.kt` | Access control on GET ports |
| **P0** | 3 | `SecurityService.kt` | Replaced `return true` with assessor/requestor/respondent checks |
| **P0** | 4 | `McpDelegationService.kt` | Username delegation now validates user's email domain |
| **P0** | 5 | `DemandController.kt` | ADMIN-only approval, approver audit trail, self-approval block |
| **P2** | 11 | `DemandController.kt` | Requestor derived from auth; ownership checks on update; ADMIN-only delete |
| **P2** | 12 | `RiskController.kt` | Class-level `@Secured("ADMIN", "RISK", "SECCHAMPION")` |
| **P2** | 13 | `ImportController.kt` | Role restrictions on 4 import endpoints (ADMIN/REQADMIN, ADMIN/VULN) |

Now let me also address your P3 message. What specific P3 vulns did you want fixed? (The message was `& fix vuls with prio 3 (` — it appears truncated.)

Let me review the P3 vulns from the audit:

| Vuln | Description | File |
|------|-------------|------|
| 14 | JSON injection in OAuth redirect URL | `OAuthController.kt:176` |
| 15 | Temp passwords use non-crypto PRNG | `ConfigBundleService.kt:777` |
| 19 | JWT token in login/OAuth response bodies | `AuthController.kt:120`, `OAuthController.kt:239` |
| 20 | SMTP password partially logged | `EmailService.kt:246` |
| 21 | SSRF via admin-configurable IdP URLs | `IdentityProviderController.kt:177-317` |
| 22 | Raw exception messages returned to clients | 40+ controller endpoints |
| 23 | Alignment reviewer token exposed in API | `AlignmentController.kt:237` |

    66→    @Get("/authorize/{providerId}")
    67→    fun authorize(@PathVariable providerId: Long, request: HttpRequest<*>): HttpResponse<*> {
    68→        return try {
    69→            logger.info("=== OAuth Authorization Request START ===")
    70→            logger.info("Provider ID: {}", providerId)
    71→            logger.info("Backend Base URL (from config): {}", backendBaseUrl)
    72→            logger.info("Request URI: {}", request.uri)

    75→            // Log whether a stale auth cookie is present (helps diagnose new-user failures)

    77→            if (hasExistingCookie) {

    79→            }

    81→            val authUrl = oauthService.buildAuthorizationUrl(providerId, backendBaseUrl)

    83→            if (authUrl != null) {

   115→        request: HttpRequest<*>

   125→            }
   126→
   127→            // Check for OAuth error
   128→            if (error != null) {

   130→                val errorMessage = when (error) {
   131→                    "access_denied" -> "Access was denied. Please try again if you wish to log in."
   132→                    "unauthorized_client" -> "OAuth application is not properly configured. Please contact support."
   133→                    "invalid_request" -> "Invalid OAuth request. Please try logging in again."
   134→                    "unsupported_response_type" -> "OAuth configuration error. Please contact support."
   135→                    "invalid_scope" -> "Requested permissions are not available. Please contact support."
   136→                    "server_error" -> "OAuth provider encountered an error. Please try again later."
   137→                    "temporarily_unavailable" -> "OAuth service is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later."
   138→                    else -> "OAuth authentication failed. Please try again."
   139→                }

   142→
   143→            // Validate required parameters

   147→                    if (state.isNullOrBlank()) "MISSING" else "present(${state?.take(10)}...)")

   149→            }
   150→
   151→            logger.info("Looking up OAuth state: {}...", state.take(10))

   159→                logger.error("Possible causes: state never saved, state expired (>10min), or state already consumed by another callback")

   161→            }
   162→
   163→            val oauthState = stateOpt.get()
   164→            logger.info("OAuth state validated: providerId={}, createdAt={}, expiresAt={}",
   165→                oauthState.providerId, oauthState.createdAt, oauthState.expiresAt)
   166→

   185→                    HttpResponse.redirect<Any>(URI.create(redirectUrl))
   186→                        .cookie(authCookieService.createAuthCookie(result.token))

   188→
   189→                is OAuthService.CallbackResult.Error -> {

   194→        } catch (e: Exception) {

   205→            HttpResponse.redirect<Any>(URI.create("$frontendBaseUrl/login?error=${java.net.URLEncoder.encode(errorMsg, "UTF-8")}"))
   206→        }
   207→    }
   208→
   209→

    21→
    22→@Controller("/api/auth")
    23→open class AuthController(
    24→    private val userRepository: UserRepository,

    28→) {

    30→    private val passwordEncoder = BCryptPasswordEncoder()
    31→
    32→    @Serdeable

    36→    )

    38→    @Serdeable

    42→        val email: String,

    45→    )

    47→    @Serdeable

    53→    )

    55→    @Post("/login")
    56→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS)
    57→    @Transactional
    58→    open fun login(@Body loginRequest: LoginRequest): HttpResponse<*> {
    59→        if (loginRequest.username.isBlank() || loginRequest.password.isBlank()) {
    60→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to "Username and password are required"))
    61→        }
    62→        
    63→        // Validate username format
    64→        val usernameValidation = inputValidationService.validateName(loginRequest.username, "Username")
    65→        if (!usernameValidation.isValid) {
    66→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to usernameValidation.errorMessage))
    67→        }
    68→        
    69→        // Validate password length (basic check, don't reveal too much)
    70→        if (loginRequest.password.length > 200) {
    71→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf("error" to "Invalid credentials"))
    72→        }
    73→        
    74→        // Check for potential injection attempts
    75→        if (inputValidationService.containsCommandInjection(loginRequest.username) ||
    76→            inputValidationService.containsPathTraversal(loginRequest.username)) {
    77→            // Log the attempt but return generic error
    78→            return HttpResponse.unauthorized<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "Invalid credentials"))
    79→        }
    80→
    81→        val userOptional = userRepository.findByUsername(loginRequest.username)
    82→        
    83→        if (userOptional.isEmpty) {
    84→            return HttpResponse.unauthorized<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "Invalid credentials"))
    85→        }
    86→
    87→        val user = userOptional.get()
    88→        
    89→        if (!passwordEncoder.matches(loginRequest.password, user.passwordHash)) {
    90→            return HttpResponse.unauthorized<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "Invalid credentials"))
    91→        }
    92→
    93→        // Generate JWT token

    96→            "username" to user.username,
    97→            "email" to user.email,
    98→            "roles" to user.roles.map { it.name },
    99→            "iss" to "secman-backend-ng",
   100→            "userId" to user.id.toString()
   101→        )
   102→        
   103→        val tokenOptional = tokenGenerator.generateToken(userDetails)
   104→        
   105→        if (tokenOptional.isEmpty) {
   106→            return HttpResponse.serverError<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "Failed to generate token"))
   107→        }
   108→
   109→        // Update last login timestamp
   110→        user.lastLogin = Instant.now()
   111→        userRepository.update(user)
   112→
   113→        val token = tokenOptional.get()
   114→
   115→        val response = LoginResponse(
   116→            id = user.id!!,
   117→            username = user.username,
   118→            email = user.email,
   119→            roles = user.roles.map { it.name },
   120→            token = token  // Still included for backward compatibility during transition
   121→        )
   122→
   123→        // Set JWT in HttpOnly secure cookie (primary auth mechanism)
   124→        return HttpResponse.ok(response).cookie(authCookieService.createAuthCookie(token))
   125→    }
   126→
   127→    @Post("/logout")
   128→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
   129→    fun logout(): HttpResponse<*> {
   130→        // Clear the auth cookie by setting an expired cookie
   131→        return HttpResponse.ok(mapOf("message" to "Logged out successfully"))
   132→            .cookie(authCookieService.createLogoutCookie())

   139→    @Post("/clear-session")

   142→        return HttpResponse.ok(mapOf("message" to "Session cleared"))
   143→            .cookie(authCookieService.createLogoutCookie())
   144→    }
   145→
   146→    @Get("/status")
   147→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
   148→    fun status(authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<*> {
   149→        val username = authentication.name
   150→        val userOptional = userRepository.findByUsername(username)
   151→
   152→        if (userOptional.isEmpty) {
   153→            return HttpResponse.unauthorized<Any>().body(mapOf("error" to "User not found"))
   154→        }
   155→
   156→        val user = userOptional.get()

   158→            id = user.id!!,
   159→            username = user.username,
   160→            email = user.email,
   161→            roles = user.roles.map { it.name }
   162→        )
   163→
   164→        return HttpResponse.ok(response)
   165→    }

1→package com.secman.exception
     2→
     3→import io.micronaut.context.annotation.Primary
     4→import io.micronaut.context.annotation.Requires
     5→import io.micronaut.http.HttpRequest

    10→import jakarta.validation.ConstraintViolationException
    11→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    12→
    13→/**
    14→ * Global exception handler for validation errors
    15→ *
    16→ * Intercepts ConstraintViolationException thrown by @Valid annotation
    17→ * and logs detailed validation failure information before returning HTTP 400.
    18→ *
    19→ * Purpose: Diagnose 400 Bad Request errors during CrowdStrike import
    20→ * Feature: 032-servers-query-import (debugging support)
    21→ *
    22→ * @Primary annotation resolves bean conflict with Micronaut's built-in ConstraintExceptionHandler
    23→ */
    24→@Produces
    25→@Singleton
    26→@Primary
    27→@Requires(classes = [ConstraintViolationException::class])
    28→class ValidationExceptionHandler : ExceptionHandler<ConstraintViolationException, HttpResponse<*>> {
    29→
    30→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(ValidationExceptionHandler::class.java)
    31→
    32→    override fun handle(request: HttpRequest<*>, exception: ConstraintViolationException): HttpResponse<*> {
    33→        // Extract all constraint violations with detailed information
    34→        val violations = exception.constraintViolations.map { violation ->
    35→            val path = violation.propertyPath.toString()
    36→            val message = violation.message
    37→            val invalidValue = violation.invalidValue?.let { value ->
    38→                when {
    39→                    value is String && value.length > 100 -> "${value.take(100)}... (${value.length} chars)"
    40→                    value is String && value.isBlank() -> "\"\" (blank string)"
    41→                    else -> value.toString()
    42→                }
    43→            } ?: "null"
    44→
    45→            "$path: $message (value: $invalidValue)"
    46→        }
    47→
    48→        // Log detailed validation failure
    49→        log.error("Validation failed for {} {}: {} violation(s) found",
    50→            request.method,
    51→            request.uri,
    52→            violations.size
    53→        )
    54→
    55→        violations.forEach { violation ->
    56→            log.error("  - {}", violation)
    57→        }
    58→
    59→        // Return user-friendly error response
    60→        return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf(
    61→            "error" to "Validation failed",
    62→            "message" to "Request contains ${violations.size} validation error(s)",
    63→            "violations" to violations
    64→        ))
    65→    }
    66→}

content.text:
Good pattern to follow. For the systemic `e.message` leakage, creating a catch-all exception handler is the most effective fix — it will prevent any unhandled exceptions from leaking internals. However, many controllers catch exceptions themselves and include `e.message` in their response. The right approach is: (1) add a catch-all handler for unhandled exceptions, and (2) for the individual controller catch blocks, they'll need per-file fixes which is a very large scope.

The most impactful and minimal fix is adding a catch-all exception handler that intercepts any `Exception` that bubbles up unhandled. For the existing controller-level catches, those are a code quality issue that should be addressed incrementally. Let me create the handler.

**Fix 14 (JSON injection)**: String interpolation for JSON is a classic vulnerability. `"""{"username":"${user}"}"""` breaks if `user` contains `"`, producing malformed/injectable JSON. Jackson's `ObjectMapper.writeValueAsString()` properly escapes all special characters per RFC 7159, making injection impossible.

**Fix 19 (Token in response body)**: The HttpOnly cookie is the correct auth delivery mechanism — it's invisible to JavaScript, preventing XSS-based token theft. Returning the token in the JSON body defeats this protection entirely. The frontend was already fully migrated to cookie-based auth (all `authToken` references are cleanup code), so removing the token from responses has no functional impact.

**Fix 21 (SSRF)**: OAuth endpoint URLs (`tokenUrl`, `userInfoUrl`, `jwksUri`) are fetched server-side during login flows. Without validation, an admin could point these at cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254) or internal services. The fix blocks private RFC 1918 ranges, link-local, localhost, and known cloud metadata IPs, while requiring HTTPS.

**Fix 22 (Exception messages)**: The `GenericExceptionHandler` acts as a safety net — any exception that escapes controller-level catches will be logged server-side but return a generic message to the client. This is defense-in-depth; the per-controller catches still need incremental cleanup but won't leak internals for unhandled cases.
